# aigateway_deployment_group (Resource)

Load-balances one model across multiple provider deployments (multi-region / multi-provider) with a strategy, retry policy and cooldown/health config.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Load-balance gpt-5.4 across two Azure regions, weighted, with a retry policy.
resource "aigateway_deployment_group" "gpt_5_4" {
  model_id = aigateway_model.gpt_5_4.model_id
  strategy = "weighted_random"

  deployments = [
    {
      provider_id       = aigateway_provider.azure_swedencentral.id
      provider_model_id = "gpt-5.4"
      deployment_name   = "gpt-5.4"
      weight            = 70
    },
    {
      provider_id       = aigateway_provider.azure_westeurope.id
      provider_model_id = "gpt-5.4"
      deployment_name   = "gpt-5.4"
      weight            = 30
      priority          = 1
    },
  ]

  retry_policy = {
    max_retries           = 3
    total_timeout_seconds = 120
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `deployments` (Attributes List) Provider deployments that serve this model. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--deployments))
- `model_id` (String) The model this deployment group belongs to.

### Optional

- `cooldown_config` (Attributes) Cooldown / health config for the group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cooldown_config))
- `retry_policy` (Attributes) Retry policy for the group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--retry_policy))
- `strategy` (String) Load-balancer strategy: round_robin | weighted_random | latency_based | least_busy. Defaults to round_robin.

<a id="nestedatt--deployments"></a>
### Nested Schema for `deployments`

Required:

- `provider_id` (String) ID of the aigateway_provider serving this deployment.
- `provider_model_id` (String) Model id as the upstream provider knows it.

Optional:

- `deployment_name` (String) Azure deployment name (azure_openai).
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this deployment is enabled. Defaults to true.
- `priority` (Number) Priority tier (0 = highest). Lower tiers used only when higher are exhausted.
- `timeout_seconds` (Number) Per-deployment timeout (overrides the model default).
- `weight` (Number) Traffic weight (1-100, higher = more traffic). Defaults to 100.


<a id="nestedatt--cooldown_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cooldown_config`

Optional:

- `base_cooldown_seconds` (Number) Base cooldown in s (default 60).
- `consecutive_error_threshold` (Number) Consecutive errors before cooldown (default 3).
- `healthy_threshold_percent` (Number) Success-rate %% above which a deployment is Healthy (default 95).
- `max_cooldown_seconds` (Number) Max cooldown in s (default 3600).
- `unhealthy_threshold_percent` (Number) Success-rate %% below which a deployment is Unhealthy (default 50).


<a id="nestedatt--retry_policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `retry_policy`

Optional:

- `backoff_base_ms` (Number) Base backoff in ms (default 500).
- `backoff_max_ms` (Number) Max backoff in ms (default 4000).
- `max_retries` (Number) Max retry attempts (default 2).
- `total_timeout_seconds` (Number) Total retry+fallback timeout in s (default 120).
